item.title: It’s all about the role of money - speech by Nathanaël Benjamin

rating.summary: Nathanaël Benjamin, Executive Director for Financial Stability Strategy and Risk at the Bank of England, presents a conceptual framework for understanding central bank policy through the three core functions of money: store of value, unit of account, and medium of exchange. He applies this framework to current financial stability topics including bank capital requirements, non-bank finance regulation, and hedge fund leverage. The speech is primarily focused on financial stability policy rather than interest rate or monetary policy decisions.
rating.stance_rationale: This speech is about financial stability frameworks and macroprudential policy, not monetary policy or interest rate decisions. There is no discussion of the rate-setting cycle, inflation outlook, or MPC deliberations.
key_quotes: Our role is to ensure trust in money.

The relevant policy question is not whether capital is good or bad for growth in a binary sense, but rather what level and structure of capital requirements most effectively support sustainable growth by appropriately balancing resilience and efficiency.
